what: Route M=64 to two-phase quant+ASM by lowering FUSED_M_THRESHOLD from 64 to 32 on top of the v211 baseline
why: Historical notes show M=64 fused is consistently slower than two-phase on BM (~13.5us fused vs ~10us two-phase), and this branch was never properly evaluated in the later cache=None baseline lineage

what: Use BLOCK_SIZE_K=384 for the M=64 fused path on top of the current baseline
why: The 64x7168x2048 branch is a major contributor and BSK=384 keeps the proven 16x128 tile family while cutting loop trips from 8 to 6, a midpoint not covered by the older BSK=256 and regressed BSK=512 tests

what: Use num_warps=2 for the M=64 fused path on top of the current baseline
why: The 64x7168x2048 branch may be slightly register-pressure limited at 4 warps; cutting to 2 keeps the tile geometry and K slicing fixed while testing a lower-pressure occupancy point that was archived but never logged
